channel.title: Comments for page "More Like Rainbow Crapitalism"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "More Like Rainbow Crapitalism"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, that was the highlight of the article for me.

Well, that and the fact that #pridehacker either didn't or couldn't actually hack the Factory's account*.

*Same with Wondertainment, but that's less funny/weird.

item.title: Re: I may be stupid
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure the hacker is "canonically" Jude, but in terms of dado appealing to the gaze, we do have this tale.

item.title: I may be stupid
item.content:encoded:
But in the middle of reading this, I started thinking:

  • Hmm, dado is an ultra-capitalist who wants to get as many customers as possible, isn't he?
  • And out of all companies in the SCPverse, dado would be the most inclined to pinkwash himself for the sake of business - however, no tweets by dado are visible.
  • But perhaps dado is smarter than we think. And his interpretation of "pinkwashing" may be to take concrete action in support of the LGBT community instead of just tweeting empty words.
  • The digging up of dirt on competitors would be great for dado's business and prove to the world that gays can trust dado.

In conclusion, dado is the #pridehacker. Don't @ me, this is a joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also for further reference, it is in extraordinarily bad taste and unnecessary to casually bring up a trans person’s dead name. Fictional or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trans women can be straight. And I don’t know. That’s a bit of a reach. You’re policing the joke of two trans people here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit late to the party, but considering one of the main POV characters in WWS is a trans woman (Faeowynn Wilson, born Felix), them using a #straightpride tweet, even as a non-canon example, does not sit well with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I want more tales about the Void social media platform.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what the fuck this is actually incredible and made me like this tale 200% more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, like Hippo said, most (cisgender) transhumanists I've met have been at least like a little transphobic - the "change your body how you like no not like that" crowd is pretty prevalent in transhumanist circles, and I think it's unrealistic to say that Anderson would be totally free of that as a company. Also, the only place I've worked where office culture wasn't moderately transphobic was literally the Human Rights Campaign, an LGBT right organization. My experience is that trans rights tend to be the first thing ignored by HR, because transphobic discrimination is pretty easy to get away with.

With all that being said, I don't think this is necessarily saying that Anderson Robotics as a corporation and everyone who works there is super transphobic. The "pride hacker" tweets are all from the perspective of an anomalous force created by Jude, an angry anti-capitalist trans man. I think it's safe to say that some things it says are exaggerated a little bit for rhetorical effect. I was intending this to be "there are some transphobic people in positions of power whose shitty behavior gets ignored because they're excellent engineers".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, the tale doesn't implicate the company as a whole; it only mentions office culture (specifically, R&D) — and describes upper management as complicit (because they don't want to lose talented engineers; that would cost them money). I feel this is thematically consistent with Anderson Robotics' themes of "progressive capitalism" and "profitable transhumanism". I'll even go so far to say that I'd find it bizarre to portray a profit-seeking corporation as anything other than opportunistic in regards to LGBTQ issues.

Also — not that anyone's claiming otherwise! — transhumanists are perfectly capable of being transphobic. Heck, in my experience, there's actually a ton of overlap! I've listened to countless people in STEM fields talk about getting cool robot arms, only to go on and say that gender expression is bullshit (disregarding science), transgender people need to "accept their bodies" (disregarding both science and their own philosophy), then make the seventy-billionth "my gender is an attack helicopter" joke. Regrettably, the fact that transhumanism as a philosophy seems perfectly suited for transgenderism doesn't make transhumanists welcoming to transgender people (I've met a few who are, but I've met a lot more who aren't).

ALL that being said: Anderson Robotics is your (Conwell's) baby in a way that most GoIs aren't. No one's written more about Anderson Robotics than you — and I think it was… 'inconsiderate'? — to assign this attribute to the company without at least discussing it with you, and how to portray it in a way that doesn't feel dissonant to all the work you've put into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so so good and so so clever and exactly what I needed after going on Twitter at any point during pride month. Pat yourself on the back. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: kinch and i decided that armand is basically anomalous dril. he just tweets about his real life and everyone online is like "lol look at this surreal humor account".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is Armand so famous in the Void? Like I'm sure he's great at being there but I feel like if anyone had to be The Big One Online it would be Esther.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, got to echo this.

Given the current set of AR content, especially that which explores Anderson interacting with rank and file employees, having the company as a whole suddenly be massively transphobic not only comes out of the blue, but is fairly out of character.

But that's, just, like, my opinion, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is so fucking funny

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: its debatable whether wondertainment even has any employees to abuse

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded:
I've always preferred to imagine that Mr. Dark is a cishet white guy. It fits my headcanon view on MC&D the best. I like to imagine them as everything wrong with major corporations plus black magic.

I've also headcanonned that she's on hormonal contraception purely to regulate and reduce menstruation, since that seems like something she would do, but I've never had an appropriate way to bring that up.

She's Iris Dark, she has no need of hormonal contreceptives. She probably has a perfect contraceptive powered by torturing baby monkeys or something.

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded:
I believe I've written about Iris Dark more than anyone else, and my Iris (I like to call her Dread Iris) is biosex female and, regardless of sexual orientation, has the sex drive of HP Lovecraft. Literally (long story). She's doesn't seek out sex or romance, but is not fundamentally opposed to the idea if there was a pragmatic reason for her to do so. I've also headcanonned that she's on hormonal contraception purely to regulate and reduce menstruation, since that seems like something she would do, but I've never had an appropriate way to bring that up.

She's also the Chief of Paratech Development and Acquisition/Junior Partner/Board Member/Darke's Proxy, not the COO. And she wears turtlenecks exclusively, so that photo's not her. I don't think My Iris, or my version of MC&D in general, would actually try pinkwashing since their clientele leans hard to the right and they know it.

None the less, still loved this. Hardy +1.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Don't worry. I got dado covered. Sit tight.

At ch00bakka's request,

Nice.

also this article is superb and I love its take on lampooning pink capitalism, along with the wonderful urban fantasy craziness. upvoted before it hit +69 tho, alas

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded:
I guess I reacted like that because Iris Dark is a pretty big character (When MC&D is involved.), and seeing her as a Transgender lesbian made my brain go "Oh, was that canon or made for this?" I also get that I overreacted a bit with the 'overboard' part with it effecting just one character.

Also wording this is hard for me, just making sure no one gets the wrong idea.

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded:
i should note that every single character i write is gay and trans unless i say they're not. iris dark here is actually conforming to the norm in my writing, rather than deviating from it.

this is like 69% joke btw dont go reading into it too much

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded: Not to mention in addition to the above that the content of the site was, and continues to be, predominantly cishet. Almost exclusively during the first few Series, and only less so since.

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded:
I think I get what you mean, and don't take the following as me lobbing an accusation at you or expressing any hostility — but I do feel it's worth noting that "going a little overboard" re: LGBTQ things often ends up becoming "having them exist at all".

Tokenism is a thing in fiction, and retroactively making characters queer can run the risk of hitting the same problems this very article is criticizing! But consider: Just this one minor change (which serves a solid narrative purpose — contrasting her identity and "wokeness" with her willingness to literally harvest the souls of queer people) is enough to prompt an aside about not going "a little too overboard".

Again, don't take this as me wagging my finger at you! I think there's validity in concerns about things like tokenism and "performative wokeness". But it's also important to remember that we often demand that even the mildest forms of LGBTQ representation justify their existence.

ETA: Plus, if anything, this is negative representation! I mean, she's a transgender lesbian running a company that harvests the souls of queer people for profit.

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded: OK thanks for the answers. While I'm fine with LGBT things, I'm might have a problem if it gets a little too overboard.

item.title: Re: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded:
1. It's for the same reasons that corporations make pride tweets in real life, which is to say, a little bit of both depending on the corporation.
2. In the world of this article, she is. I can't make a blanket statement about all Iris Darks, nor would I want to.

item.title: I've got two questions
item.content:encoded:
1. Is most of this for actual support, lying for profit or a bit of both?
2. Is Iris Dark really trans?

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Does dado even know what Twitter is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lmao i actually do use light mode on twitter, consider me called out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't say anything bad. I was asking what it is. So whatever you say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[45/45]

This really got me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I don't think the SCP Foundation is actually very well-known for producing 'what-if- scenarios, but this is very much the kind of speculative fiction I'd daydream about while staring out of a window, or possibly while running angrily on the treadmill. I do appreciate the faux-Twitter, it's really cool how they have this entire network of their own - and one that maybe even the FOUNDATION doesn't know about, although I'd hope they do. Otherwise, God help them, Christ.

I really like how it pointed out all the flaws behind 'rainbow capitalism', as you put it, or arguably 'soulless pride'. It made it really purposeful, between calling out CMD's actions towards their employees directly or pointing out the other horrors they committed by virtue of being CM&D, even with Dark's identity.

But I think the thing that 100% made it for me was this:

"⁂the_factory i hope everyone retweeting this doesn't know what the factory actually is. it isn't joking about the overseer drones. this isn't a funny parody account, it's a malevolent extradimensional parasite, and y'all should stop giving it a platform."

This is the most SCP Foundation-y quote I could ever come across, and I fucking love it! It is also, terrifying, in some ways - but it's utterly mundane in relatable ways, and I think that's fucking fantastic.

I look forward to more SCPs from you, or at least rereading this and daydreaming about such a device - even if its concept is a little bit chilling, it's only by virtue of taking place in this corporate virtual hellscape, and I think that's really cool.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: I mean, it's not Dr. Wondertainment's fault Ms. Sweetie turned out evil. He made 20 Little Misters and 18 were good, that's a 90% success rate for Little Misters being nonevil.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Please don’t use Sweetie as evidence for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really pretty good. My only qualm is that I don't know how I feel about Anderson Robotics being labeled as transphobic since that company literally builds transhumans, but the rest of this is really really good.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Not even a history of hiring trans people, since Sweety wasn't hired. She was made. Given the option, Dr. Wondertainment made a trans woman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How insightful.

God willing, some staff member will be by shortly to remind you that contentless comments on articles like these aren't kosher.

The Factory tweets were the best as they didn't even sound human, it sounded like something an eldritch abomination that doesn't fully understand humans and is disguised as a corporation would write.

I also like the implication that Dr. Wondertainment doesn't have any trans/homophobic past. Which makes sense as they have a long history of hiring trans people. Though on the flip side one could argue their still exploiting Pride Month to sell more candy that shoots the blood out of your body.

But, alas, this tale has a major flaw. We didn't get to see what dado's reaction was.

0/10 for lack of dado.

Now I want to write a Pride themed Wondertainment SCP with them supporting lgbt stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Grunkle Stan voice) Now, this? This is beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe it’s from the Oneiroi format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You just had to make the obvious joke with Jude's tweet, didn't you? :|

+1, and I am so going to steal the tweet format at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how Wondertainment is the only one who isn't called out. Because they're perfect (or, well, at least not LGBT+phobic).

+1, obviously, though I kind of wish we had more from The Factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and here i thought the weeders were too cool for tweeting!

(this is an official callout post for whoever's using light-mode anomaTwitter)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lmao this owns

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wtf is this shit..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a work of fucking beauty.

item.title: Licensing Post
item.content:encoded:
This is really anal-retentive, but:

100% noncommercial parody

It's not 100% non-commercial, as CC-BY-SA 3.0 explicitly allows for commercial use, meaning commercial use based on this article is possible. However, that does not change the overall point that it is the image is fair use, so it may remain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me, it was the callout tweet suggesting we de-platform a malevolent extra-dimensional parasite trying to appear woke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ongoing stream of MC&D tweets interrupting the narrative with brief twitter-sized chunks of a monologue describing their abominable human rights record is what took this way over the top for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is tied with Portland Derby Teams for the funniest thing on the site. You're a golden comedy author and I love this.

The bill for my funeral will be in the mail, I have died from laughter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm proud of how good and attractive we are as writers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so i started writing this as a bit, just to get funny screencaps of MCD and the Factory pinkwashing themselves, and then i decided to actually make it a tale, and then Kinch came up with the idea that gave it a plot instead of being just some funny tweets, so here we are. this tale is dedicated to queer liberation and the anticapitalist agenda; i hope it makes some people angry.

Read SCP-4493 for the continuation of the story.

the tweet template is originally from TyGently's Intermission

tweet template
– hide block




Wilson's Wildlife Solutions
⁂wilsonswild


What about #StraightPride?
  • ECHOES 0
  • HUMS 0


2:12 AM - 7 June 2019


 [[div style="background-color: #ffffff; float: center; border: grey 4px; width: 370px; padding: 10px; margin: auto; background-color: light gray; box-shadow: 2px 2px 2px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.1);"]] [[span style="float:left; margin-left:5px; margin-top:12px; background-color: #ffffff; padding: 0px 10px;"]] [[image http://scp-wiki.wdfiles.com/local--files/groups-of-interest/WWS width="50px"]] [[/span]] @@ @@ [[size 1.5em]] ##grey|Wilson's Wildlife Solutions ⁂wilsonswild## [[/size]] @@ @@ @@ @@ [[size 1.5em]]What about ##blue|#StraightPride##? [[/size]] @@ @@ * [[size .8em]] ##grey|ECHOES## [[/size]][[size 1.1em]] ##blue| 0## [[/size]] * [[size .8em]] ##grey|HUMS## [[/size]][[size 1.1em]] ##blue| 0## [[/size]] @@ @@ [[size .8em]] ##grey|2:12 AM - 7 June 2019## [[/size]] [[/div]] @@ @@
image credits
have fun

lesbiangengar from https://www.deviantart.com/mortymoon/art/Gengar-Pokemon-567166751 and https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lipstick_Lesbian_flag_without_lips.svg
iris from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:SF_(15034693491).jpg
bluntfiend from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:User_(89620)_-_The_Noun_Project.svg
harmpit from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:WikiVoc-banana.svg
polaricecraps from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:CIBER_NASA_Iceberg_analogy_PIA18850.jpg
arewezuulyet from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FXC17_Stay_Puft.jpg
syzygy from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sun_and_Moon_Nuremberg_chronicle.jpg
jockjams from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Brass_Razoo.jpg
begone liberal from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:SubMarcosHorseFromAfar.jpg
elvendragqueen from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Marihuana_Leaf.jpg
postoptimusprime from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Anarcho-Transhumanist_flag_with_logo.svg and https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Trans_harrotasunaren_bandera.png
goi images from http://www.scp-wiki.net/groups-of-interest
rainbow flag from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gay_Pride_Flag.svg
joyce rand from intermission
and the absolut image is a screencap from absolut's twitter that i fucked with in inspect element, i'm pretty sure this is 100% fair use since it's 69% noncommercial parody, let me know if it's not kosher and we'll figure something out
